---
name: 💡 Feature Request
about: Suggest an idea for Image Harvest
title: '[Feature] '
labels: enhancement
---

## The Problem

What are you trying to accomplish that the current version doesn't let you do? Describe the real-world scenario.

> Good example: "I download reference images from 20+ Pinterest boards a week. Each download creates a separate folder. I'd love a way to merge them into one session."
>
> Bad example: "Add folder merge feature."

## Proposed Solution

If you have an idea of how this could work, describe it. A rough UI sketch or a short user flow is very welcome.

## Current Workaround

How are you working around this today? (If there's no workaround, say so.)

## Who Else Benefits?

Help me understand if this is a niche need or a broader one:

- [ ] Mostly just me / my specific workflow
- [ ] Probably useful for users with similar jobs (designers / researchers / e-commerce / etc.)
- [ ] Useful for most Image Harvest users

## Scope Check (Please Read)

Image Harvest intentionally stays focused. To keep the extension fast and the codebase maintainable, features are more likely to be accepted if they:

- ✅ Improve the core loop: **extract → filter → download**
- ✅ Work 100% locally (no new backend calls)
- ✅ Don't require a new permission in `manifest.json`
- ✅ Don't add runtime dependencies

Features are unlikely to be accepted if they:

- ❌ Drift away from the "image tool" focus (e.g., video download, PDF extraction)
- ❌ Require tracking, telemetry, or remote code
- ❌ Duplicate functionality better served by another tool

Please say which side you think your request lands on, and why.

## Additional Context

Screenshots, links to similar features in other tools, or anything else.
